name: Poblado ibérico de San Antonio y Tossal Redó — circular desde Calaceite
description: Hike a moderate 8.2-mile (13.1 km) circular trail from Calaceite to explore ancient Iberian settlements and panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Calaceite offers diverse natural landscapes within the Matarraña region of Teruel, Aragón. The area is characterized by the Matarraña and Algars river valleys, providing scenic routes through Mediterranean olive, almond, and vine groves, as well as pine forests. Ancient Iberian settlements are situated on hills, offering panoramic views of the surrounding terrain. The region features a network of trails suitable for various abilities. Best hiking trails around Calaceite The most popular hiking route is Poblado ibérico de San Antonio y Tossal Redó — circular desde Calaceite, an 8.2 miles (13.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to ancient Iberian settlements and offers panoramic views of the region. Another top favourite among local hikers is San Antonio Iberian Settlement and San Cristóbal Hill Trail, an easy 2.0 miles (3.3 km) path. This trail ascends San Cristóbal Hill, providing views of Calaceite and its surroundings. Local hikers also love the Matarraña Valleys Trail (PR-Z 162), a 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail leading through the scenic Matarraña river valleys, often completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Hiking around Calaceite is defined by river valleys, ancient Iberian settlements, and diverse agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Calaceite are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Calaceite's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Calaceite?
A: There are over 30 hiking trails around Calaceite, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix of easy, moderate, and challenging routes to explore the diverse landscapes of the Matarraña region.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families with children?
A: Yes, Calaceite offers several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1323747491/san-antonio-iberian-settlement-and-san-cristobal-hill-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Antonio Iberian Settlement and San Cristóbal Hill Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.3 km path offering views of Calaceite. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169689584/matarrana-valleys-trail-pr-z-162" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matarraña Valleys Trail (PR-Z 162)&lt;/a&gt; is another easy option, covering 6.0 km through scenic river valleys. The Vía Verde de la Val de Zafán, a converted railway line, is also excellent for families and easy walks.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Hiking around Calaceite offers a variety of natural attractions. You can explore the scenic Matarraña and Algars river valleys, discover ancient Iberian settlements, and enjoy panoramic views from hills like San Cristóbal. Nearby, you might encounter impressive features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4961316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Estrets Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and the natural pools of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4959065" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toll Blau&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also known for its Mediterranean olive, almond, and vine groves.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Calaceite area?
A: Yes, circular routes are available for those who prefer to start and end in the same location. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1168833093/poblado-iberico-de-san-antonio-y-tossal-redo-circular-desde-calaceite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poblado ibérico de San Antonio y Tossal Redó — circular desde Calaceite&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 13.2 km trail that takes you to ancient Iberian settlements with panoramic views.

Q: What are the best seasons for hiking in Calaceite?
A: The best seasons for hiking in Calaceite are typically spring and autumn. During these times, the weather is mild and pleasant, and the landscapes are vibrant with spring blooms or rich autumn colors. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, focusing on shaded routes or river areas. Winter offers crisp air and fewer crowds, though some higher routes might require appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any hiking trails that offer great viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Calaceite lead to elevated points offering spectacular views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1168833093/poblado-iberico-de-san-antonio-y-tossal-redo-circular-desde-calaceite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poblado ibérico de San Antonio y Tossal Redó — circular desde Calaceite&lt;/a&gt;, for example, provides panoramic vistas of the region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1323747491/san-antonio-iberian-settlement-and-san-cristobal-hill-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Antonio Iberian Settlement and San Cristóbal Hill Trail&lt;/a&gt; ascends San Cristóbal Hill, offering excellent views of Calaceite and its surroundings.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Calaceite?
A: The hiking routes in Calaceite are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the well-maintained paths, and the blend of natural beauty with historical sites like the Iberian settlements. Many appreciate the options available for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest to explore while hiking?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. Several hiking routes lead to ancient Iberian settlements, such as the San Antonio Iberian Settlement and Tossal Redó. The town of Calaceite itself has a medieval heritage. Further afield, you can explore charming villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1563297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arnes&lt;/a&gt;, which also serves as a starting point for trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6930354" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toll Blau – Arnes Historic Center loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is it possible to access hiking trailheads around Calaceite using public transport?
A: While Calaceite is a rural area, public transport options might be limited directly to all trailheads. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to Calaceite itself. For many routes, especially those starting directly from the town, walking from the center is feasible. For more remote trailheads, a car might be necessary, and parking is generally available near popular starting points.

Q: Are there any more challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced trekkers seeking more challenging routes, Calaceite and its surroundings offer options. There are 4 difficult trails in the area. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19815967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Estrets d'Arnes – Toll Blau loop from Arnes&lt;/a&gt;, which is an 18.3 km difficult route. The nearby Puertos de Beceite Natural Park also provides rugged limestone peaks and extensive trail networks for more ambitious hikes.

Q: Can I find trails with water features like rivers or natural pools?
A: Yes, the Matarraña and Algars river valleys are central to the region's natural beauty. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169689584/matarrana-valleys-trail-pr-z-162" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matarraña Valleys Trail (PR-Z 162)&lt;/a&gt; follow these scenic rivers. The Pesquera route (along the Ulldemó river) and the Parrizal route (along the Matarraña river) are known for their spectacular landscapes and opportunities to see natural pools, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4959065" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toll Blau&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Calaceite dog-friendly?
A: Generally, many hiking trails in the Calaceite region are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the outdoors with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to check for any specific local regulations or protected areas that might have restrictions. Always carry water for your dog and clean up after them.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: The hike is estimated to take approximately 3.5 hours to complete, covering a distance of about 13.1 kilometers. This duration allows time to explore the historical sites along the way.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the trail?
faqs.answer: Komoot classifies this trail as moderate. While a good physical condition is beneficial, the paths are generally accessible and suitable for hikers of varying experience levels.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary attractions are the ancient Iberian settlements. You'll explore the San Antonio Iberian Settlement and the Tossal Redó Archaeological Site. Both offer fascinating insights into the region's history and provide a Panoramic view of Calaceite and the surrounding Matarraña region.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail takes you through historical and natural landscapes. You'll encounter strategic hills where the Iberian settlements are located, offering expansive views. The sites themselves feature well-preserved urban layouts with cobbled streets and remains of houses.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Calaceite?
faqs.answer: Since the trail is circular from Calaceite, you can typically find parking within the town. It's advisable to look for public parking areas in Calaceite, especially near the starting point of the loop.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through historical sites or near livestock, and to ensure you clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Matarraña region generally offers pleasant hiking conditions in spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, while winter might be cooler but still enjoyable on clear days.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the trail or the Iberian settlements?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the trail itself or to visit the open-air Iberian settlements of San Antonio and Tossal Redó.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the El Camí IT6TR4 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat, Carreró del Tossal, Carrer dels Freginals, and Camino de la Font de Noro.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities or places to eat near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Calaceite, where you'll find various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options to enjoy before or after your hike. It's also recommended to visit the Juan Cabré Museum in Calaceite for more context on the Iberian sites.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and historical sites, sturdy shoes are recommended. Bring enough water, especially during warmer months, and snacks. A camera is also a good idea to capture the panoramic views and archaeological remains.
